low_score: you're paying hundreds in annual fees and using approximately none of the benefits. your credit cards are basically expensive bookmarks. at least bookmarks are useful.

you have more unused benefits than a gym membership in february. the difference is the gym costs $30 a month, your cards cost ten times that. and you use neither.
somewhere a bank executive is buying a boat with your annual fees. you could've at least used the uber credit first. that's fifteen dollars a month just sitting there.
your capture rate says you'd save money by cutting up all your cards and getting a debit card from a local credit union. i'm not even joking. do the math.

half your benefits are used, half are forgotten. you're like someone who bought a gym membership and only uses the shower. technically getting value, but come on.
your wallet score is mediocre. you're leaving hundreds on the table while successfully redeeming the easy stuff. the streaming credit doesn't make you an optimizer, it makes you a netflix subscriber.
you're in the danger zone — just good enough to not downgrade, just bad enough to not feel good about it. pick a side. either optimize or stop pretending.

your optimization is terrifying. you've used benefits most people don't even know exist. the hotel credit, the equinox thing, the saks credit — you're basically working a part-time job managing credit cards.
